form.name: Name
form.nameHint: Operator-facing name for this execution target.
form.descriptionHint: Optional note about what this machine is for.
form.driverHint: Local runs on this host. SSH stores a remote machine target. Sandbox stores plugin-backed provider config on the shared environment seam.
form.sshHostHint: DNS name or IP address for the remote machine.
form.sshRemoteWorkspaceHint: Absolute path that Paperclip will verify during SSH connection tests.
form.sshPrivateKeyHint: Optional PEM private key. Leave blank to rely on the server's SSH agent or default keychain.
form.sshKnownHostsHint: Optional known_hosts block used when strict host key checking is enabled.
form.sshStrictHostKeyHint: Keep this on unless you deliberately want probe-time host key acceptance disabled.
form.sandboxProviderHint: Installed run-capable sandbox provider plugins appear here.
form.sandboxNoConfig: This provider does not declare additional configuration fields.

adapterSupport.environmentChoices: Environment choices use the same adapter support matrix as agent defaults. SSH is always available for remote-managed adapters, and sandbox environments appear only when a run-capable sandbox provider plugin is installed.
adapterSupport.notAdapterTypes: These are not adapter types. They back the Sandbox driver for adapters that support sandbox execution.

viewer.label: Viewer

admin.label: Admin
admin.description: Recommended for operators who need to invite people, create agents, and approve joins.
admin.gets: Can create agents, invite users, assign tasks, and approve join requests.

owner.label: Owner
owner.description: Full company access, including membership and permission management.
owner.gets: Everything in Admin, plus managing members and permission grants.

projectWorkspaceDetail.title: Workspace

sourceType.localGitCheckoutDesc: A local path Paperclip can use directly.
sourceType.localNonGitPathDesc: A local folder without git semantics.
sourceType.remoteGitRepoDesc: A repo URL with optional refs and local checkout.
sourceType.remoteManagedWorkspaceDesc: A hosted workspace tracked by external reference.

projectWorkspaceDetail.notFound: Workspace not found for this project.
projectWorkspaceDetail.description: Configure the concrete workspace Paperclip attaches to this project. These values drive per-workspace checkout behavior, default runtime services for child execution workspaces, and let you override setup or cleanup commands when one workspace needs special handling.
projectWorkspaceDetail.primaryHint: This is the project's primary codebase workspace.
projectWorkspaceDetail.advancedRuntimeJsonDesc: Paperclip derives Services and Jobs from this JSON. Prefer editing named commands first; use raw JSON for advanced lifecycle, port, readiness, or environment settings.
projectWorkspaceDetail.servicesAndJobsDesc: Long-running services stay supervised here, while one-shot jobs run on demand against this workspace. Execution workspaces inherit this config unless they override it.
projectWorkspaceDetail.disabledHint: Project workspaces need a working directory before local commands can run, and services also need runtime config.

hint.setupCommand: Runs when this workspace needs custom bootstrap
hint.cleanupCommand: Runs before project-level execution workspace teardown
hint.workspaceCommandsJson: Execution workspaces inherit this config unless they override it. Legacy `services` arrays still work, but `commands` supports both services and jobs.

empty.servicesNotStarted: No services have been started for this workspace yet.
empty.noCommandConfig: No workspace command config is defined for this workspace yet.
empty.noJobs: No one-shot jobs are configured for this workspace yet.
empty.noFiles: No files in this workspace yet.

runtimeAction.serviceStopped: Workspace service stopped. Issue execution is not paused.
runtimeAction.serviceRestarted: Workspace service restarted. Issue execution is not paused.

validation.commandsJsonMustBeObject: Workspace commands JSON must be a JSON object.
validation.remoteManagedRequiresRefOrUrl: Remote-managed workspaces require a remote workspace ref or repo URL.
validation.requiresPathOrUrl: Workspace requires at least one local path or repo URL.
validation.localPathMustBeAbsolute: Local workspace path must be absolute.
validation.repoUrlMustBeValid: Repo URL must be a valid URL.

uploadFile.title: Upload File
uploadFile.dropzone: Drop file here or click to select

deleteConfirm.title: Delete workspace?
deleteConfirm.description: This will permanently delete the workspace and all its contents.

routineDetail.title: Routine

toast.routineChangedBody: Someone else updated this routine. Reload to see the latest revision.
toast.saveFailedBody: Paperclip could not save the routine.
toast.runFailedBody: Paperclip could not start the routine run.
toast.updateFailedBody: Paperclip could not update the routine.
toast.triggerSavedBody: The routine cadence update was saved.
toast.agentRequiredBody: Set a default agent before enabling routine automation.

banner.agentRequired: Default agent required. This routine can stay as a draft and still run manually, but automation stays paused until you assign a default agent.

routineDetail.outOfDateBody: This routine changed while you were editing. Reload to merge the latest revision before saving again.

secrets.description: Routine secrets apply to every issue this routine creates. They override matching keys in project and agent env.

concurrencyPolicy.coalesce_if_active: Keep one follow-up run queued while an active run is still working.
concurrencyPolicy.always_enqueue: Queue every trigger occurrence, even if several runs stack up.
concurrencyPolicy.skip_if_active: Drop overlapping trigger occurrences while the routine is already active.

catchUpPolicy.skip_missed: Ignore schedule windows that were missed while the routine or scheduler was paused.
catchUpPolicy.enqueue_missed_with_cap: Catch up missed schedule windows in capped batches after recovery.

signingModeDescriptions.bearer: Expect a shared bearer token in the Authorization header.
signingModeDescriptions.hmac_sha256: Expect an HMAC SHA-256 signature over the request using the shared secret.
signingModeDescriptions.github_hmac: Accept GitHub-style X-Hub-Signature-256 header (HMAC over raw body, no timestamp).
signingModeDescriptions.none: No authentication — the webhook URL itself acts as a shared secret.

secretMessage.saveNow: Save this now. Paperclip will not show the secret value again.

executionWorkspaceDetail.title: Execution Workspace
executionWorkspaceDetail.advancedRuntimeJsonDesc: Override the inherited workspace command model only when this execution workspace truly needs different service or job behavior.
executionWorkspaceDetail.disabledHint: Execution workspaces need a working directory before local commands can run, and services also need runtime config.

validation.commandsJsonMustBeObject: Workspace commands JSON must be a JSON object.
validation.repoUrlMustBeValid: Repo URL must be a valid URL.

empty.servicesNotStarted: No services have been started for this execution workspace yet.
empty.noCommandConfig: No workspace command config is defined for this execution workspace yet.
empty.noJobs: No one-shot jobs are configured for this execution workspace yet.

settings.title: Workspace settings
settings.description: Edit the concrete path, repo, branch, provisioning, teardown, and runtime overrides attached to this execution workspace.

hint.provisionCommand: Runs when Paperclip prepares this execution workspace
hint.teardownCommand: Runs when the execution workspace is archived or cleaned up
hint.workspaceCommandsJson: Legacy `services` arrays still work, but `commands` supports both services and jobs.

runtimeConfig.overrides: This execution workspace currently overrides the project workspace runtime config.
runtimeConfig.inheriting: This execution workspace is inheriting the project workspace runtime config.
runtimeConfig.none: No runtime config is currently defined on this execution workspace or its project workspace.

routines.title: Workspace routines
routines.description: Routines that use workspace-specific variables can be run against this execution workspace.
routines.empty: No routines use workspace-specific variables yet.

routineStarted.body: Paperclip created a run using this execution workspace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Plugin

instanceGeneralSettings.description: Configure instance-wide preferences including log display, keyboard shortcuts, backup retention, and data sharing.

deployment.title: Deployment and auth
deployment.deploymentLocal: Local trusted mode is optimized for a local operator. Browser requests run as local board context and no sign-in is required.
deployment.deploymentPublic: Authenticated public mode requires sign-in for board access and is intended for public URLs.
deployment.deploymentPrivate: Authenticated private mode requires sign-in and is intended for LAN, VPN, or other private-network deployments.

censorUsername.title: Censor username in logs
censorUsername.description: Hide the username segment in home-directory paths and similar operator-visible log output. Standalone username mentions outside of paths are not yet masked in the live transcript view. This is off by default.

keyboardShortcuts.title: Keyboard shortcuts
keyboardShortcuts.description: Enable app keyboard shortcuts, including inbox navigation and global shortcuts like creating issues or toggling panels. This is off by default.

backupRetention.title: Backup retention
backupRetention.description: Configure how long automatic database backups are retained. Backups run roughly every hour and are compressed with gzip. Within the daily window all backups are kept; beyond that, one backup per week and one per month are preserved.

aiFeedback.title: AI feedback sharing
aiFeedback.description: Control whether thumbs up and thumbs down votes can send the voted AI output to Paperclip Labs. Votes are always saved locally.
aiFeedback.noDefault: No default is saved yet. The next thumbs up or thumbs down choice will ask once and then save the answer here.
aiFeedback.dontAllowDesc: Keep voted AI outputs local only.
aiFeedback.retestHint: To retest the first-use prompt in local dev, remove the feedbackDataSharingPreference key from the instance_settings.general JSON row for this instance, or set it back to "prompt". Unset and "prompt" both mean no default has been chosen yet.

signOut.title: Sign out
signOut.description: Sign out of this Paperclip instance. You will be redirected to the login page.

approvalDetail.title: Approval
approvalDetail.approvalConfirmedDescription: Requesting agent was notified to review this approval and linked issues.
approvalDetail.linkedIssuesDescription: Linked issues remain open until the requesting agent follows up and closes them.
approvalDetail.resolveBudgetFromCosts: Resolve this budget stop from the budget controls on {{path}}.
approvalDetail.confirmDeleteAgent: Delete this disapproved agent? This cannot be undone.

routines.title: Routines
routines.description: Automate recurring work with schedules and triggers.

empty.selectCompany: Select a company to view routines.

composer.title: New routine
composer.subtitle: Define the recurring work first. Default project and agent are optional for draft routines.

advanced.title: Advanced delivery settings
advanced.subtitle: Keep policy controls secondary to the work definition.

composer.footer: After creation, Paperclip takes you straight to trigger setup. Draft routines stay paused until you add a default agent.

toast.createdBodyWithAgent: Add the first trigger to turn it into a live workflow.
toast.createdBodyDraft: Draft saved. Add a default agent before enabling automation.
toast.updateFailedBody: Paperclip could not update the routine.
toast.runFailedBody: Paperclip could not start the routine run.
toast.agentRequiredBody: Set a default agent before enabling routine automation.

concurrencyPolicy.coalesce_if_active: If a run is already active, keep just one follow-up run queued.
concurrencyPolicy.always_enqueue: Queue every trigger occurrence, even if the routine is already running.
concurrencyPolicy.skip_if_active: Drop new trigger occurrences while a run is still active.

catchUpPolicy.skip_missed: Ignore windows that were missed while the scheduler or routine was paused.
catchUpPolicy.enqueue_missed_with_cap: Catch up missed schedule windows in capped batches after recovery.

usage.empty: No active bindings. Add this secret in agent, project, environment, or plugin config to start using it.

events.empty: No access events recorded yet. Each runtime resolution writes a redacted entry here.

uploadHint.selectCompany: Select a company first to store avatars.

profileSettings.avatarUploadHint: Upload a new profile photo or drag and drop an image.
profileSettings.displayNameHint: Your display name appears in the board interface and on your profile.

instanceAccess.title: Instance Access
instanceAccess.description: Search users, manage instance-admin status, and control which companies they can access.

error.noAccess: Instance admin access is required to manage users.

instanceAccess.selectUser: Select a user to inspect instance access.

section.companyAccessDescription: Toggle company membership for this user. New access defaults to an active operator membership.

instanceExperimentalSettings.title: Experimental
instanceExperimentalSettings.description: Opt into features that are still being evaluated before they become default behavior.

environments.title: Enable Environments
environments.description: Show environment management in company settings and allow project and agent environment assignment controls.

isolatedWorkspaces.title: Enable Isolated Workspaces
isolatedWorkspaces.description: Show execution workspace controls in project configuration and allow isolated workspace behavior for new and existing issue runs.

autoRestartDevServer.title: Auto-Restart Dev Server When Idle
autoRestartDevServer.description: In `pnpm dev:once`, wait for all queued and running local agent runs to finish, then restart the server automatically when backend changes or migrations make the current boot stale.

autoRecovery.title: Auto-Create Issue Recovery Tasks
autoRecovery.description: Let the heartbeat scheduler create recovery issues for issue dependency chains found inside the configured lookback window.

errors.invalidHours: Lookback hours must be a whole number from 1 to 720.

dialog.title: Confirm auto-recovery
dialog.description: {{count}} recovery task{{count === 1 ? '' : 's'}} match the last {{hours}} hours.
dialog.noTasks: No recovery tasks would be created right now. Auto-recovery can still run for future liveness incidents in this window.
dialog.skipped: {{count}} current finding{{count === 1 ? '' : 's'}} outside the configured lookback and will not be touched.

inbox.title: Inbox
inbox.emptyDescription: Mentions and direct messages will appear here.

markAllReadConfirm.title: Mark all as read?
markAllReadConfirm.description: This will mark {{count}} unread {{itemLabel}} as read.

clearAllConfirm.title: Clear all tasks?
clearAllConfirm.description: All {{count}} manual tasks will be archived. This cannot be undone.

successfulRunHandoff.required: Paperclip needs a disposition before this issue can continue.
successfulRunHandoff.exhausted: Paperclip could not resolve this issue's missing disposition automatically. The issue is blocked on a recovery owner.

sidebarAccountMenu.viewProfileDesc: Open your activity, task, and usage ledger.

backlog.willNotWake: will not wake until moved to

interaction.willBeSkipped: will be skipped if you accept this interaction
interaction.createdIssuesSummary: Created {{created}} issues and skipped {{skipped}} during review
interaction.placeholderReason: Add a short reason for rejecting this suggestion
interaction.optionalRevision: Optional: what would you like revised?
interaction.optionalChange: Optional: tell the agent what you'd change.
interaction.requestNotResolved: This request could not be resolved. Try again or create a new request.
interaction.question: Question {{number}}
interaction.submitHint: Submit once after you finish the full form

help.name: Display name for this agent.
help.title: Job title shown in the org chart.
help.role: Organizational role. Determines position and capabilities.
help.reportsTo: The agent this one reports to in the org hierarchy.
help.capabilities: Describes what this agent can do. Shown in the org chart and used for task routing.
help.adapterType: How this agent runs: local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP webhook.
help.cwd: Deprecated legacy working directory fallback for local adapters. Existing agents may still carry this value, but new configurations should use project workspaces instead.
help.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep this small and dynamic. Use it for current-task framing, not large static instructions. Supports {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }} and other template variables.
help.model: Override the default model used by the adapter.
help.thinkingEffort: Control model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
help.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
help.dangerouslySkipPermissions: Run unattended by auto-approving adapter permission prompts when supported.
help.dangerouslyBypassSandbox: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Required for filesystem/network access.
help.search: Enable Codex web search capability during runs.
help.fastMode: Enable Codex Fast mode. This burns credits/tokens much faster and is supported on GPT-5.4 and manual Codex model IDs.
help.workspaceStrategy: How Paperclip should realize an execution workspace for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, or use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
help.workspaceBaseRef: Base git ref used when creating a worktree branch. Leave blank to use the resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
help.workspaceBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
help.worktreeParentDir: Directory where derived worktrees should be created. Absolute, ~-prefixed, and repo-relative paths are supported.
help.runtimeServicesJson: Optional workspace runtime service definitions. Use this for shared app servers, workers, or other long-lived companion processes attached to the workspace.
help.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum number of agentic turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
help.command: The command to execute (e.g. node, python).
help.localCommand: Override the path to the CLI command you want the adapter to call (e.g. /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
help.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.bootstrapPrompt: Only sent when Paperclip starts a fresh session. Use this for stable setup guidance that should not be repeated on every heartbeat.
help.payloadTemplateJson: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payloads before Paperclip adds its standard wake and workspace fields.
help.webhookUrl: The URL that receives POST requests when the agent is invoked.
help.heartbeatInterval: Run this agent automatically on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
help.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run can take before being terminated. 0 means no timeout.
help.graceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing the process.
help.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignments, API calls, UI actions, or automated systems.
help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum number of heartbeat runs that can execute simultaneously for this agent.
help.maxTurnContinuationEnabled: Automatically queue bounded continuation runs when an adapter stops because its per-run turn cap was exhausted.
help.maxTurnContinuationMaxAttempts: Maximum automatic continuations after one max-turn stop. This is separate from max turns per run.
help.maxTurnContinuationDelaySec: Seconds to wait before starting each max-turn continuation.
help.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending limit in cents. 0 means no limit.

finance.financeLedgerDesc: Account-level charges that do not map to a single inference request.
finance.debitMinusCredit: Debit minus credit for the selected period
finance.estimatedDebitsDesc: Estimated debits that are not yet invoice-authoritative
finance.selectDateRange: Select a start and end date to load data.
finance.inferenceLedgerDesc: Request-scoped inference spend for the selected period.
finance.monthlyBudgetConsumed: {{percent}}% of monthly budget consumed in this range.
finance.byAgentDesc: What each agent consumed in the selected period.
finance.byProjectDesc: Run costs attributed through project-linked issues.
finance.budgetControlPlaneDesc: Hard-stop spend limits for agents and projects. Provider subscription quota stays separate and appears under Providers.
finance.activeIncidentsDesc: Open soft or hard threshold crossings
finance.pendingApprovalsDesc: Budget override approvals awaiting board action
finance.resolveIncidentsDesc: Resolve hard stops here by raising the budget or explicitly keeping the scope paused.
finance.agentBudgetDesc: Recurring monthly spend policies for individual agents.
finance.projectBudgetDesc: Lifetime spend policies for execution-bound projects.
finance.noBudgetPolicies: No budget policies yet. Set agent and project budgets from their detail pages, or use the existing company monthly budget control.
finance.noCostEventsInPeriod: No cost events in this period.
finance.noBillableEventsInPeriod: No billable events in this period.
finance.byBillerDesc: Account-level financial events grouped by who charged or credited them.
finance.noFinanceEventsYet: No finance events yet. Add account-level charges once biller invoices or credits land.
finance.accountLevelCharges: Account-level charges grouped by event type.
finance.noFinanceEvents: No financial events during this period.
finance.topUpsFeesCredits: Top-ups, fees, credits, commitment charges, and other non-request fees.

issueRecoveryActionCard.unassigned: unassigned — pick one to wake them

headlines.missingDisposition: This issue's run finished, but no next step was chosen.
headlines.strandedAssignedIssue: Paperclip retried this issue's last run and it still has no live execution path.
headlines.activeRunWatchdog: The active run has been silent. Recovery is observing without interrupting it.
headlines.issueGraphLiveness: Paperclip detected this issue lost a live action path. A recovery owner needs to act.

tryAgain.label: Try again
tryAgain.description: Dismiss recovery and return the source issue to todo.

markDone.label: Mark issue done
markDone.description: Restore by recording the requested work as complete.

sendForReview.label: Send for review
sendForReview.description: Hand off to a reviewer with a real review path.

falsePositiveDone.label: False positive, done
falsePositiveDone.description: Dismiss recovery and mark the source issue complete.

falsePositiveReview.label: False positive, review
falsePositiveReview.description: Dismiss recovery and send the source issue for review.

footerMessages.observing: Recovery is observing without interrupting the live run.
footerMessages.staysOpen: The card stays open until an explicit decision is recorded.

outputFeedback.feedbackSharingDesc: Choose whether voted AI outputs can be shared with Paperclip Labs. This answer becomes the default for future thumbs up and thumbs down votes.
outputFeedback.voteSavedLocally: This vote is always saved locally.
outputFeedback.changeLater: You can change this later in Instance Settings > General.

pathInstructions.title: How to get a full path
pathInstructions.description: Paste the absolute path (e.g. /Users/you/project) into the input field.
pathInstructions.macosStep1: Open Finder and navigate to the folder.
pathInstructions.macosStep3: Hold the Option (⌥) key — "Copy" changes to "Copy as Pathname".
pathInstructions.macosStep4: Click "Copy as Pathname", then paste here.
pathInstructions.macosTip: You can also open Terminal, type cd, drag the folder into the terminal window, and press Enter. Then type pwd to see the full path.
pathInstructions.windowsStep1: Open File Explorer and navigate to the folder.
pathInstructions.windowsStep2: Click in the address bar at the top — the full path will appear.
pathInstructions.windowsStep3: Copy the path, then paste here.
pathInstructions.windowsTip: Alternatively, hold Shift and right-click the folder, then select "Copy as path".
pathInstructions.linuxStep1: Open a terminal and navigate to the directory with cd.
pathInstructions.linuxStep2: Run pwd to print the full path.
pathInstructions.linuxStep3: Copy the output and paste here.
pathInstructions.linuxTip: In most file managers, Ctrl+L reveals the full path in the address bar.

documentDiff.sameRevision: Both sides are the same revision.

envVarEditor.createSecretFromValue: Create secret from current plain value
envVarEditor.storeValueAsSecret: Store value as secret and replace with reference
envVarEditor.runsWillFailUntilRemap: Runs will fail until you remap or re-enable.
envVarEditor.setEnvVarDescription: Set KEY to the env var name the process expects, for example GH_TOKEN. Choose Secret to resolve a stored value at run start. PAPERCLIP_* variables are injected automatically.

executionWorkspaceClose.disposableWorkspace: This workspace is disposable and can be archived.
executionWorkspaceClose.cleanupFailedRetry: Cleanup previously failed on this workspace. Retrying close will rerun the cleanup flow and update the workspace status if it succeeds.

issueColumns.statusDesc: Issue state chip on the left edge.
issueColumns.projectDesc: Linked project pill with its color.
issueColumns.workspaceDesc: Execution or project workspace used for the issue.

successfulRunHandoff.title: This issue still needs a next step.
successfulRunHandoff.description: A run finished successfully, but this issue is still open in <0>in_progress</0> with no clear owner for the next action.
successfulRunHandoff.tip2: Send it for review or ask for input.
successfulRunHandoff.tip3: Mark it blocked with a blocker owner.
successfulRunHandoff.tip4: Delegate follow-up work or queue a continuation.
successfulRunHandoff.correctiveWakeFallback: Corrective wake queued for the assignee

issueBlockedNotice.stalledMulti: Work on this issue is blocked by {{label}}, but the chain is stalled in review without a clear next step. Resolve the stalled reviews below or remove them as blockers.
issueBlockedNotice.stalledSingle: Work on this issue is blocked by {{label}}, but the chain is stalled in review without a clear next step. Resolve the stalled review below or remove it as a blocker.
issueBlockedNotice.blockedBySingle: Work on this issue is blocked by {{label}} until it is complete. Comments still wake the assignee for questions or triage.
issueBlockedNotice.blockedByMulti: Work on this issue is blocked by {{label}} until they are complete. Comments still wake the assignee for questions or triage.
issueBlockedNotice.blockedStatus: Work on this issue is blocked until it is moved back to todo. Comments still wake the assignee for questions or triage.
issueBlockedNotice.recoveryTooltip: {{label}} — open the source issue to act.

feedbackDialog.title: Save your feedback sharing preference
feedbackDialog.description: Choose whether voted AI outputs can be shared with Paperclip Labs. This answer becomes the default for future thumbs up and thumbs down votes.
feedbackDialog.localOnly: This vote is always saved locally.
feedbackDialog.instruction: Choose <0>Always allow</0> to share this vote and future voted AI outputs. Choose <1>Don't allow</1> to keep this vote and future votes local.
feedbackDialog.changeLater: You can change this later in Instance Settings > General.

properties.envAppliedDescription: Applied to each run of issues in this project. Project values override conflicting keys in agent environment variables.
properties.codebaseHelpTooltip: The repo identifies the real source. Local folder is where the agent writes code by default.
properties.additionalLegacyWorkspaces: Additional legacy workspace records exist for this project. Paperclip is using the primary workspace as the codebase view.
properties.localFolderMustBeAbsolutePath: Local folder must be a full absolute path.
properties.repoMustBeValidGitHubUrl: Repo must use a valid GitHub or GitHub Enterprise repository URL.
properties.clearLocalFolderFromWorkspace: Clear local folder from this workspace?

properties.archiveDescription: Archive this project to hide it from sidebar and project pickers.
properties.unarchiveDescription: Unarchive this project to restore visibility in sidebar and project pickers.
properties.executionWorkspacesTooltip: Isolated issue checkout and execution workspace behavior defaults owned by the project.
properties.enableIsolatedIssueCheckoutsDesc: Let issues choose between project main checkout and isolated execution workspace.
properties.newIssuesDefaultToIsolatedCheckout: New issues default to isolated checkout
properties.newIssuesDefaultToIsolatedCheckoutDesc: If disabled, new issues stay on project main checkout unless someone opts in.
properties.provisionTeardownDescription: Provision runs in the derived worktree before executing the agent. Teardown commands are stored here for future cleanup flows.

config.capabilitiesPlaceholder: Describe what this agent can do...
config.promptTemplatePlaceholder: You are agent {{ agent.name }}. Your role is {{ agent.role }}...
config.promptTemplateWarning: Prompt template is sent on every heartbeat. Keep it concise and dynamic to avoid repeated token costs and cache thrashing.
config.defaultEnvironmentHint: Agent-level default execution target. Project and issue settings can still override this.
config.noModelDetected: No model detected. Please select or enter manually.
config.opencodeDiscoveryNote: Real-time OpenCode model discovery only runs in local environments. Using curated list for {{environment}} with manual entry.
config.noModelDetectedYet: No model detected yet. Enter provider/model.
config.bootstrapPromptPlaceholder: Optional initial setup prompt for first run
config.bootstrapPromptWarning: Bootstrap prompt is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Suggest moving content to the agent's prompt template or instructions.
config.codexMinimalThinkingWarning: Codex may reject 'minimal' thinking mode when search is enabled.
config.canCreateNewAgentsDescription: Lets this agent create or hire agents and implicitly assign tasks.

taskAssignHint.agentCreator: Enabled automatically while this agent can create new agents.
taskAssignHint.explicitGrant: Enabled via explicit company permission grant.

help.name: Display name for this agent.
help.title: Position shown in org chart.
help.role: Organizational role. Determines placement and capabilities.
help.reportsTo: Who this agent reports to in the org hierarchy.
help.capabilities: Describe what this agent can do. Displayed in org chart, used for task routing.
help.adapterType: How this agent runs: Local CLI (Claude/Codex/OpenCode), OpenClaw Gateway, spawned process, or generic HTTP Webhook.
help.cwd: Deprecated alternate working directory for local adapters. Existing agents may still have this value, but new configs should use project workspaces.
help.promptTemplate: Sent on every heartbeat. Keep small and dynamic. Used for current task framing, not large static instructions. Supports template variables like {{ agent.id }}, {{ agent.name }}, {{ agent.role }}.
help.model: Override default model used by adapter.
help.thinkingEffort: Controls model reasoning depth. Supported values vary by adapter/model.
help.chrome: Enable Claude's Chrome integration by passing --chrome.
help.dangerouslySkipPermissions: Run unattended by auto-approving adapter permission prompts (if supported).
help.dangerouslyBypassSandbox: Run Codex without sandbox restrictions. Requires filesystem/network access.
help.search: Enable Codex web search during runs.
help.fastMode: Enable Codex fast mode. Consumes credits/tokens faster, only supported on GPT-5.4 and manual Codex model IDs.
help.workspaceStrategy: How Paperclip should implement execution workspaces for this agent. Keep project_primary for normal cwd execution, use git_worktree for issue-scoped isolated checkouts.
help.workspaceBaseRef: Base git reference to use when creating worktree branches. Leave empty to use resolved workspace ref or HEAD.
help.workspaceBranchTemplate: Template for naming derived branches. Supports {{issue.identifier}}, {{issue.title}}, {{agent.name}}, {{project.id}}, {{workspace.repoRef}}, and {{slug}}.
help.worktreeParentDir: Directory where derived worktrees are created. Supports absolute paths, ~-prefixed paths, and repo-relative paths.
help.runtimeServicesJson: Optional workspace runtime services definition. Used for shared application servers, worker processes, or other long-running companion processes attached to the workspace.
help.maxTurnsPerRun: Maximum agent turns (tool calls) per heartbeat run.
help.localCommand: Override CLI command path for adapter invocation (e.g., /usr/local/bin/claude, codex, opencode).
help.extraArgs: Extra CLI arguments for local adapters, comma-separated.
help.envVars: Environment variables injected into the adapter process. Use plain values or secret references.
help.bootstrapPrompt: Sent only when Paperclip starts a new session. Used for stable setup guidance that shouldn't repeat on every heartbeat.
help.payloadTemplateJson: Optional JSON merged into remote adapter request payload before Paperclip adds standard wake and workspace fields.
help.webhookUrl: URL to receive POST requests (when agent is invoked).
help.heartbeatInterval: Automatically run this agent on a timer. Useful for periodic tasks like checking for new work.
help.timeoutSec: Maximum seconds a run may execute before termination. 0 means no timeout.
help.graceSec: Seconds to wait after sending interrupt before force-killing process.
help.wakeOnDemand: Allow this agent to be woken by assignment, API calls, UI actions, or automation systems.
help.cooldownSec: Minimum seconds between consecutive heartbeat runs.
help.maxConcurrentRuns: Maximum heartbeat runs this agent may execute simultaneously.
help.maxTurnContinuationEnabled: Automatically queue limited continuation runs when adapter stops due to per-turn limit exhaustion.
help.maxTurnContinuationMaxAttempts: Maximum automatic continuation attempts after one max-turn stop. Separate from max turns per run.
help.maxTurnContinuationDelaySec: Seconds to wait before starting each max-turn continuation.
help.budgetMonthlyCents: Monthly spending cap (in cents). 0 means unlimited.

choosePathDialog.title: Specify path manually
choosePathDialog.description: Browser security restrictions prevent the app from reading full local paths via file picker. Copy the absolute path and paste it into the input.
choosePathDialog.macosStep2: Hold Option and right-click the folder.
choosePathDialog.macosStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
choosePathDialog.windowsStep1: Locate the folder in File Explorer.
choosePathDialog.windowsStep2: Hold Shift and right-click the folder.
choosePathDialog.windowsStep4: Paste the result into the path input.
choosePathDialog.terminalStep3: Copy the output and paste into the path input.

instructionsBundle.modeTooltip: Managed: Paperclip stores and serves the instructions bundle. External: you provide a path on disk where the instructions live.
instructionsBundle.rootPathTooltip: The absolute directory on disk where the instructions bundle lives. In managed mode this is set by Paperclip automatically.
instructionsBundle.entryFileTooltip: The main file the agent reads first when loading instructions. Defaults to AGENTS.md.

empty.selectCompany: Select a company to review join requests.
empty.noRequests: No join requests match the current filters.

error.noPermission: You do not have permission to review join requests for this company.

inviteUxLab.title: Invite and signup UX review surface
inviteUxLab.description: This page collects the current invite landing, signup, approval-result, and company invite-management states in one place so styling changes can be reviewed without recreating each backend condition by hand.

highlights.inviteLoading: Invite loading, access-check, missing-token, and unavailable states
highlights.inlineAccountCreation: Inline account creation and sign-in variants, including feedback/error copy
highlights.humanAgentTransitions: Human accept, agent request, and auto-accept transitions
highlights.pendingApprovalResults: Pending approval, joined-now, claim secret, and onboarding result screens
highlights.companyInviteManagement: Company invite creation, copied-link, history, empty, and permission-error states

section1.title: Landing state coverage
section1.description: Small cards for the fast-return invite states that do not render the full split-screen layout.

section2.title: Split-screen invite flows
section2.description: These frames mirror the production invite surface closely enough to review spacing, hierarchy, and control states while keeping data fixture-driven.

section3.title: Approval and completion screens
section3.description: These are the post-submit states returned from invite acceptance, including optional claim and onboarding metadata.

section4.title: Auth page states
section4.description: The general `/auth` page uses a different composition from invite landing. These previews keep both sign-in and sign-up variants visible.

section5.title: Company invite management
section5.description: This section captures the board-side invite creation flow, copied-link state, audit table, and the edge states that are otherwise tedious to stage.

loading.title: Loading invite
loading.body: Shown while invite summary, deployment mode, or auth session data is still loading.

checkingAccess.title: Checking your access
checkingAccess.body: Shown after sign-in while the app verifies whether the current user already belongs to the invited company.

invalidToken.title: Invalid invite token
invalidToken.body: The token is missing entirely, so the page short-circuits before any invite lookup.

notAvailable.title: Invite not available
notAvailable.body: Used for expired, revoked, already-consumed, or otherwise missing invites.

bootstrapComplete.title: Bootstrap complete
bootstrapComplete.body: Result screen for bootstrap CEO invites after setup has been accepted successfully.

autoAccept.title: Auto-accept in progress
autoAccept.body: Signed-in human users skip the extra button click and move straight into join submission.

alreadyMember.title: Already a member
alreadyMember.body: Acceptance stays disabled and the page redirects into the company once membership is confirmed.

inviteResult.title: Invite result surfaces
inviteResult.body: Both pending-approval and joined-now confirmations are included below with claim and onboarding extras.

inviteSummary.signUpFirst: Create your Paperclip account first. If you already have one, switch to sign in and continue the invite with the same email.
inviteSummary.reviewInvite: Your account is ready. Review the invite details, then accept it to continue.
inviteSummary.submitAgentInfo: Review the invite details, then submit the agent information below to start the join request.

inlineAuth.accountExists: An account already exists for jane@example.com. Sign in below to continue with this invite.

joinedNow.title: You joined the company

visitSettings.description: Ask them to visit Company Settings → Access to approve your request.

pathInstructions.macosStep4: Click "Copy as Pathname", then paste it here.
pathInstructions.macosTip: You can also open Terminal, type cd, drag the folder into the Terminal window, and press Enter. Then type pwd to see the full path.

pathInstructions.windowsStep2: Click the address bar at the top — the full path appears.
pathInstructions.windowsStep3: Copy the path, then paste it here.
pathInstructions.windowsTip: Alternatively, hold Shift and right-click the folder, then select "Copy as path".
pathInstructions.linuxStep1: Open Terminal and navigate to the directory with cd.
pathInstructions.linuxStep2: Run pwd to print the full path.
pathInstructions.linuxStep3: Copy the output and paste it here.
pathInstructions.linuxTip: In most file managers, Ctrl+L shows the full path in the address bar.

routineHistory.firstRevisionDescription: Revision 1 is the only history this routine has. Saving an edit creates the first additional revision.
routineHistory.restoringCreatesNewRevision: Restoring this revision creates a new revision {{nextRevisionNumber}} with the same content.
routineHistory.unsavedEditsWarning: You changed {{fieldsText}} but haven't saved yet. Save or discard before previewing or restoring an older revision.
routineHistory.title: Title
routineHistory.webhookSecretsNotStored: Webhook secrets are not stored in revisions. If a restored webhook trigger needs re-creation, Paperclip mints fresh secret material at restore time.
routineHistory.restoreRevisionDescription: This creates a new revision {{newNumber}} with the same content as revision {{targetNumber}}. Revisions {{targetNumber}}–{{currentRevisionNumber}} stay in history and are not modified.
routineHistory.routineFieldsWillRevert: Routine field values, variables, and schedule cron will revert.
routineHistory.webhookTriggerWillBeRecreated: The webhook trigger {{label}} will be recreated with a new URL and secret.
routineHistory.changeSummaryPlaceholder: Why are you restoring? Visible in history.
routineHistory.triggerStateRestored: Trigger enabled state was restored from the snapshot.
routineHistory.triggerStateRestoredWithSecrets: Trigger enabled state was restored from the snapshot. New webhook secrets are available in the banner above.
routineHistory.couldNotRestoreRevision: Paperclip could not restore the revision.

routineRunVariablesDialog.description: Choose the agent and optional project for this one run. Routine defaults are prefilled and won't be changed.
routineRunVariablesDialog.defaultAgentRequired: Default agent required for this run.
routineRunVariablesDialog.chooseExistingWorkspace: Choose an existing workspace before running.

routineVariablesEditor.detectedFromPlaceholders: Detected from `{{name}}` placeholders in the routine title and instructions.
routineVariablesEditor.promptUserForValue: Prompt the user for this value before each manual run.
routineVariablesEditor.label: Label
routineVariablesEditor.usePlaceholdersInInstructions: Use `{{variable_name}}` placeholders in the instructions to prompt for inputs when the routine runs.
routineVariablesEditor.howToPromptForInputs: How to prompt for inputs and which variables Paperclip fills in automatically.
routineVariablesEditor.customVariablesDescription: Type `{{variable_name}}` anywhere in the title or instructions. Paperclip detects each placeholder, lists it under Variables, and prompts for a value before each run.
routineVariablesEditor.namesMustStartWithLetter: Names must start with a letter and may use letters, numbers, and underscores.
routineVariablesEditor.pickTypeDefaultValueRequired: Pick a type (text, textarea, number, boolean, select), default value, and whether it is required.
routineVariablesEditor.sameNameReusedIsOneVariable: The same name reused across the title and instructions is treated as one variable.
routineVariablesEditor.builtInVariablesDescription: These are filled in automatically — no setup needed and they will not appear in the Variables list.

sidebarAgents.agentWasPausedByBudgetLimits: Agent was paused by budget limits

hints.defaultEnvironment: The default environment for this agent's runs. Leave empty for company default.

warnings.promptTemplate: This is a local agent - the prompt template is stored in your local workspace.

config.canCreateNewAgentsDescription: Allow this agent to create new sub-agents.

taskAssignHint.ceoRole: The CEO can assign tasks to any agent.
taskAssignHint.agentCreator: This agent can assign tasks to agents it creates.
taskAssignHint.explicitGrant: This agent has explicit permission to assign tasks.

config.apiKeysDescription: API keys allow this agent to authenticate calls to the Paperclip server.
config.cheapModelDescription: Used when a run requests the cheap profile (e.g. routine summaries). The primary model stays unchanged.
config.noExplicitCheapModelSelected: No explicit cheap model selected — runtime falls back to {{model}}.

label.issue_feedback_vote_saved: saved feedback on an AI output
label.issue_monitor_escalated_to_board: escalated a monitor to the board
label.issue_successful_run_handoff_required: Run finished without a clear next step
label.issue_successful_run_handoff_escalated: Run finished without a next step - recovery escalated
